question_text: A petrol tanker truck becomes negatively charged as it moves along a motorway due to friction with the air. Explain why this could be dangerous when the truck is refuelling at a petrol station, and describe what happens to electrons during this process.
correct_answer: The negatively charged truck has excess electrons. When refuelling, if the truck suddenly discharges (loses electrons rapidly) to the ground or petrol pump, a spark could be created. This spark could ignite the flammable petrol vapours, causing a fire or explosion. During the discharge, electrons rapidly flow from the truck (which has excess) to the ground (which has fewer electrons), creating the dangerous spark.
explanation: Static electricity on a tanker truck is dangerous because excess electrons can suddenly discharge as a spark when the truck connects to the ground or pump. Since petrol vapours are highly flammable, this spark could cause a serious fire. Grounding cables allow electrons to flow safely and slowly rather than creating a dangerous spark.
